amdp-hana-012.interviewScriptHinglish: Sir, S/4HANA mein AMDP aur HANA optimization first-class citizens hain. Simplification ke karan tables jaise ACDOCA — Universal Journal — bahut bade ho gaye hain jisme finance ki saari posting consolidated hai. Aise tables pe aggregation pure ABAP mein efficient nahi, isliye SAP ne AMDP aur CDS ko strongly recommend kiya hai. Practically, mere project mein FI report ACDOCA pe AMDP se banaya — billions of rows ka GROUP BY HANA columnar engine ne seconds mein process kiya. Iska benefit yeh hai ki S/4HANA simplification — flatter data model, in-memory processing aur AMDP pushdown — sab milke real-time business reporting deliver karte hain, jo ECC mein possible nahi tha.
amdp-hana-012.interviewScriptEnglish: S/4HANA architecture is built around HANA, making AMDP and CDS the preferred ways to deliver performance. Simplification has consolidated finance into ACDOCA, sales into VBAK/VBAP variants, and inventory into MATDOC, creating very large central tables. Processing such volumes in ABAP loops is no longer viable, so SAP recommends pushdown via AMDP or CDS. HANA 2.0 features like text analytics, predictive functions, and series data can be used inside AMDP for advanced scenarios. In our migration project we converted legacy SELECT-LOOP finance reports to AMDP and saw real-time response times. The business value is faster month-end close, real-time Fiori KPIs, and the ability to retire batch aggregation jobs.

wordByWordSamjho.meaning: Universal Journal table consolidating finance, CO, ML postings in S/4HANA
Simplified material document table replacing legacy MKPF/MSEG
SAP-published catalogue of structural changes in S/4HANA
HANA layout where columns are stored together for aggregation speed
CDS annotation marking the view as CUBE, FACT, or DIMENSION
RESTful ABAP Programming model for building business services

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Reusing ECC report patterns directly on S/4HANA simplified tables
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: ECC compatibility views and legacy code patterns hide performance opportunities and may even break
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Read the simplification list and rewrite reports against ACDOCA, MATDOC and CDS with pushdown

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Designing AMDP without considering HANA columnar storage
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Wide SELECT * queries undo the benefits of column-wise compression
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: List only the needed columns and design WHERE clauses that align with columnar pruning

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Performing post-processing of AMDP results in ABAP loops
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Brings data back to the app server and negates pushdown gains
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Keep transformation, filtering, and aggregation inside the AMDP body

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Skipping CDS Table Function when Fiori needs AMDP logic
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Fiori cannot consume AMDP directly; you end up with bespoke Gateway code
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Wrap the AMDP in a CDS Table Function with @OData.publish for clean Fiori consumption

amdp-hana-012.quickRevisionNotes:
What: AMDP positioned as a first-class S/4HANA pushdown mechanism on simplified tables.

Used for:
- Finance reporting on ACDOCA at scale
- Material movement analytics on MATDOC
- Real-time KPIs backing Fiori Elements apps

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- ACDOCA, MATDOC, VBAK/VBAP, BSEG (read access)
- CDS define table function ... implemented by method
- HANA 2.0 SQLScript: predictive, series, spatial functions
- ADT in Eclipse for development, HANA Cockpit for monitoring

Interview keyword: simplified tables plus pushdown equals S/4HANA performance

amdp-hana-013.interviewScriptHinglish: Sir, AMDP aur HANA optimization mein bahut saari subtle galtiyan hoti hain jo code compile bhi ho jaata hai aur initial test pe pass bhi ho jaata hai, lekin production mein issues create karti hain. SELECT * pe columnar performance kharab hoti hai. USING bhoolne se transport fail hota hai. Authorization wrapper miss karne se security gap aata hai. Practically, mere project mein ek AMDP USING mein CDS view miss thi — dev mein chal raha tha kyunki object pehle se tha, lekin transport sequence galat hone se QA mein activation fail hua. Iska benefit yeh hai ki yeh common pitfalls pehle se samajh ke code review aur peer testing mein actively check karne se production issues bahut kam ho jaati hain.
amdp-hana-013.interviewScriptEnglish: AMDP code can compile and pass unit tests yet introduce subtle production issues. The most common pitfalls include SELECT * on wide HANA tables, missing tables in the USING clause, hardcoded client values, absent authorization checks in the calling wrapper, missing OPTIONS READ-ONLY on reporting methods, swallowed cx_amdp_error exceptions, and post-processing AMDP results in ABAP loops. Each looks harmless individually but undermines performance, security, or maintainability. In one project a missing CDS view in USING worked in dev but failed activation in QA due to transport sequence. The business value of knowing these pitfalls is reducing rework, audit findings, and production incidents.

wordByWordSamjho.meaning: Reads every column, hurting columnar HANA performance
AMDP clause naming every table or view used inside the body
Safety addition blocking DML inside an AMDP
Exception class raised by failed AMDP execution
Anti-pattern where MANDT is fixed in SQLScript rather than passed in

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Using SELECT * on wide S/4HANA tables inside AMDP
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Forces HANA to read all column segments, defeating columnar optimization
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: List only the columns you need to leverage column-store pruning

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Forgetting tables in the USING clause
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Activation passes in dev but transport breaks because dependency tracking missed it
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: List every transparent table, CDS view, and called AMDP inside USING

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Skipping AUTHORITY-CHECK in the ABAP wrapper
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: AMDP itself cannot enforce authorization, leading to unauthorized data exposure
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Run all authorization checks in the ABAP wrapper before invoking the AMDP

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Re-processing AMDP results inside ABAP LOOP
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Wastes the pushdown gains by replicating logic on the application server
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Keep transformation, aggregation, and joins inside the AMDP body

amdp-hana-013.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Catalogue of subtle AMDP mistakes that pass compilation but hurt production.

Used for:
- Code review checklists for AMDP work
- Onboarding new ABAP-on-HANA developers
- Hardening reports before transport

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- Explicit column lists, full USING clauses, OPTIONS READ-ONLY
- AUTHORITY-CHECK in ABAP wrapper, cx_amdp_error handling
- ABAP Dictionary table types for parameters
- iv_mandt parameter for client filtering

Interview keyword: production-grade discipline around AMDP

amdp-hana-014.interviewScriptHinglish: Sir, AMDP testing thoda alag hai normal ABAP se kyunki body HANA pe execute hoti hai. Standard approach hai ABAP Unit test class jisme test doubles use karke method behaviour assert karte hain. Direct HANA test ke liye SQL Analyzer ya HANA Database Explorer mein stored procedure ko sample data se execute karte hain. ST05 SQL trace se pushdown verify hota hai. Practically, mere project mein migration testing kiya — same input pe ABAP report aur naya AMDP report run karke results compare kiye, parallel run validation ki. Iska benefit yeh hai ki AMDP changes confidently transport ho sakte hain aur edge cases — NULL, empty input, large volumes — pehle se cover ho jaate hain.
amdp-hana-014.interviewScriptEnglish: Testing AMDP combines ABAP Unit testing for the wrapper logic with HANA-side validation for the SQLScript body. ABAP Unit classes can call the AMDP method with controlled inputs and assert the EXPORTING result; for cases where the framework is too heavy, the generated HANA stored procedure can be called from HANA Database Explorer or SQL Analyzer with sample data. ST05 traces confirm pushdown, and parallel comparison testing — running the old ABAP code and the new AMDP and comparing outputs — guards against migration regressions. Edge cases such as NULL inputs, empty selections, and very large volumes deserve explicit tests. The business value is confident deployments with minimal production surprises.

wordByWordSamjho.meaning: Unit-testing framework built into ABAP for class-based tests
HANA tool that visualises execution plans and runtime for SQL
HANA object created by the AMDP, executable directly in HANA tools
Performance SQL trace transaction used to verify pushdown
Running old and new logic side by side to compare results

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Testing only the ABAP wrapper and assuming the SQLScript is correct
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Hides bugs in joins, NULL handling, or aggregation that only show on real data
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Combine ABAP Unit tests with direct execution of the generated stored procedure in HANA tools

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Skipping edge-case tests for NULL or empty inputs
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: NULL propagation can corrupt aggregates and empty selections often crash callers
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Write explicit unit tests with NULL columns, empty internal tables, and boundary dates

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Not validating pushdown after migrating to AMDP
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: The query may still be executed inefficiently on HANA if join hints or types are wrong
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Capture ST05 traces and inspect PlanViz to confirm the plan matches expectations

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Releasing AMDP changes without parallel comparison
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Subtle differences vs the legacy ABAP version may go unnoticed until business complains
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Run both implementations on production-like data and reconcile every column

amdp-hana-014.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Combined ABAP-side and HANA-side testing approach for AMDP methods.

Used for:
- Regression coverage during ABAP-to-AMDP migrations
- Validating SQLScript correctness on edge cases
- Confirming pushdown post-deployment

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- ABAP Unit (CLASS ltcl_test DEFINITION FOR TESTING)
- HANA Database Explorer / SQL Analyzer for stored procedure tests
- ST05 trace, SAT runtime analysis
- Parallel run scripts comparing old and new outputs

Interview keyword: dual-layer testing with reconciliation

amdp-hana-015.interviewScriptHinglish: Sir, interviewer ke trap questions woh hote hain jo sunne mein seedhe lagte hain lekin galat direction mein le jaate hain. Jaise — 'AMDP hamesha faster hai kya?' Jawab hai nahi — chhote datasets pe overhead zyada hai. 'AMDP CDS ko replace kar sakta hai?' Nahi — dono ka purpose alag hai. 'AMDP ECC pe bhi chalta hai?' Sirf HANA-based systems pe. Practically, mere project mein junior developer ne small lookup AMDP banaya — performance kharab hui. Refactor karke Open SQL pe wapas le aaye. Iska benefit yeh hai ki interview mein limitations clearly bolne se candidate experienced lagta hai — beginner sirf benefits bolta hai, experienced trade-offs samajhta hai.
amdp-hana-015.interviewScriptEnglish: Interview trap questions on AMDP test whether the candidate understands trade-offs rather than just benefits. Common traps include claims that AMDP is always faster, that AMDP can replace CDS, that AMDP runs on any database, that AMDP with a LOOP outperforms ABAP LOOP, and that AMDP automatically uses indexes. The mature answer is that AMDP shines for high-volume pushdown but has overhead for small queries, complements rather than replaces CDS, is HANA-only, performs best with set-based SQLScript, and depends on HANA optimizer statistics for index usage. Showing awareness of these nuances signals real production experience and elevates the interview answer beyond surface-level theory.

wordByWordSamjho.meaning: Cost of invoking a stored procedure, noticeable on small data volumes
Bridge that exposes an AMDP through the CDS layer
Component that chooses execution plans and decides on index usage
Processing entire result sets in one operation, the preferred AMDP style

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Claiming AMDP is always faster than ABAP Open SQL
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Procedure call overhead dominates on small queries and may slow them down
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Use AMDP for high-volume or complex calculations; Open SQL for selective reads

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Treating AMDP and CDS as interchangeable
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: CDS is declarative with annotations and broad consumption; AMDP is procedural
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Use CDS for reusable models; AMDP for complex logic; CDS Table Function to bridge

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Saying AMDP runs on any database
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: AMDP is bound to HANA via IF_AMDP_MARKER_HDB
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Acknowledge HANA dependency; choose Open SQL for portable solutions

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Believing a LOOP inside AMDP is automatically fast
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Row-by-row processing bypasses HANA's parallel set-based engine
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Replace SQLScript LOOPs with CASE WHEN, GROUP BY, and JOINs

amdp-hana-015.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Trap questions designed to surface AMDP misconceptions during interviews.

Used for:
- Demonstrating senior-level awareness of trade-offs
- Avoiding overconfident claims about pushdown
- Differentiating AMDP, CDS, and Open SQL in conversation

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- AMDP overhead vs Open SQL on small queries
- CDS vs AMDP vs CDS Table Function decision matrix
- HANA-only via IF_AMDP_MARKER_HDB
- Set-based SQLScript over LOOP

Interview keyword: trade-offs over absolutes
